_comment: Register / memory baseline contract for the Phase 1.28 settled-title
state. Each assertion is a don't-regress check that one of the prior
phases' hardware-register fixes has not silently come undone.
- WRAM-H 0x060FF000   -> BSS upper bound monitoring; if non-zero
the stack-side may be encroaching on BSS
so R0CAOS MUST be 1 for the title scene
to render with the correct palette
_ghz_active_comment: GHZ_AUTOADVANCE_TICKS=480 so the state machine advances past
These don't-regress contracts catch the FG.TMP / SKY.DAT load-
failure bug classes that Phase 2.3k and 2.3l fixed. If any sub-
asset load regresses, g_ghz_load_error_code != 0 trips the gate.
If the title state machine fails to advance to TS_GHZ_ACTIVE (6),
either the load failed or a downstream subroutine inside
mania_load_ghz_scene crashed before the state advance.
Symbol addresses (verify via game.map after each rebuild — BSS
addresses can shift across builds; refresh both this baseline AND
tools/qa_phase2_3l_gate.py if grep game.map shows new offsets):
_phase2_4b_comment: Phase 2.4b (2026-05-28, Task #139) — collision-parity contract.
User-reported bug: 'Sonic jumps in and out of monitors'. Root cause:
ad-hoc AABB hit-tests in Entities.c with no position snap-back or
velocity zero on side contact. Fix: port decomp Player_CheckCollisionBox
(SonicMania_Objects_Global_Player.c:2267-2341 + Collision.cpp:276-487)
and dispatch all three entity classes (itembox/spring/motobug) through
it.
The post-fix don't-regress contract is enforced by the dedicated gate:
python tools/qa_phase2_4b_collision_gate.py \
--with-savestates samples/qa_phase2_4b_t0.mcs,samples/qa_phase2_4b_t1.mcs
Sub-predicates that the gate catches:
P1 — Player.c defines Player_CheckCollisionBox + Player.h declares it
+ FallbackHitbox{-10,-20,10,20} present (decomp Player.c:12).
P2 — Entities.c has >=3 Player_CheckCollisionBox call sites (one for
each of itembox/spring/motobug per decomp ItemBox.c:441,
Spring.c:144, Motobug.c via Player_CheckBadnikTouch->
hitboxBadnik).
P3 — Player state plausibility from a savestate: xpos/ypos in GHZ1
bounds, |xsp| <= topSpeed+1, |ysp| <= terminal+1. Catches
runaway physics from broken collision (e.g. unbounded xsp
accumulation from per-frame penetration with no snap-back).
P4 — Post-contact snap-back assertion. Skipped on harness-captured
savestates (Mednafen 1.32.1 boots fresh per capture, can't
bracket a live collision frame); defence in depth for user-
supplied bracketing states.
The gate is the canonical regression catcher for this bug class. No
additional don't-regress assertion added to qa_register_baseline.json
because the contract is per-frame player_t state, NOT a fixed hardware
register value (the qa_register_baseline is for hardware-register
regressions like SPCTL/TVMR/CRAOFB, per its comment block above).
_phase2_4f_comment: Phase 2.4f (2026-05-28, Task #143) — canonical Mania-Mode GHZ Act 1
spawn contract. User-reported bug: 'this doesnt seem like the canonical
first level of the game once someone selects mania mode'. Root cause:
src/mania/Game.c::mania_ghz_player_init_on_transition spawned Player at
world col 0 + forced g_ghz_autorun=true (Saturn-fit shortcut, forbidden
per memory/post-button-press-canon-scope.md).
Fix: extract Scene1.bin Player entity slot 887 (canonical x=108, y=947,
characterID=3) at build time via tools/extract_ghz_spawn.py, ship as
12-byte cd/GHZ1SPWN.BIN (big-endian i32 x, i32 y, u32 characterID),
read at TS_TRANSITION_TO_GHZ via ghz1_spawn_from_cd. Drop g_ghz_autorun
= true and g_ghz_input_grace = 30 from init_on_transition.
Don't-regress gate:
python tools/qa_phase2_4f_canonical_spawn_gate.py \
--with-savestates samples/qa_phase2_4f_t0.mcs,samples/qa_phase2_4f_t1.mcs
Sub-predicates the gate catches:
P1 — init_on_transition no longer sets g_ghz_autorun=true.
P2 — init_on_transition no longer sets nonzero g_ghz_input_grace.
P3 — cd/GHZ1SPWN.BIN x matches Scene1.bin Player slot 887 x (108)
AND init_on_transition no longer passes literal 0 as xpos arg.
P4 — cd/GHZ1SPWN.BIN y matches Scene1.bin Player slot 887 y (947)
AND init_on_transition no longer hard-codes start_y=960.
P5 — (savestate-optional) Player.xpos/ypos don't move >2px across
consecutive frames with no input asserted (no autorun motion).
Decomp authority:
- tools/_decomp_raw/SonicMania_Objects_Global_Player.c:542-672
Player_Create reads entity->position from the Scene.bin entity-
table dispatch.
- rsdkv5-src/RSDKv5/RSDK/Scene/Scene.cpp:528-665 LoadSceneAssets ->
ProcessObjects -> per-class Create.
- extracted/Data/Stages/GHZ/Scene1.bin Player hash =
md5('Player') = 636da1d35e805b00eae0fcd8333f9234, slot 887.
Saturn-port deviation: Approach B (build-time extract) chosen over
Approach A (runtime RSDK entity-table dispatch via rsdk_load_scene)
because scene_ghz currently bypasses rsdk_load_scene; Approach A
requires substantial new engine plumbing scheduled as a future
Phase A engine-completeness task. The COORD IS DECOMP-CANONICAL;
only the read path is Saturn-fit.
